nativeRange.habitat: Rocky subtidal environments, reef-associated zones; tends to tolerate shadier or deeper positions within the photic zone

decorPreferences: shaded or lower-light areas of display

imageCandidates.notes: Primary product photo from AlgaeBarn Leafy Sphere Ogo listing
Product photo from Mosaic Macros Purple Dragon's Breath (H. maculata) listing

summary: Red ornamental macroalgae with broad, blotchy blades in pink, purple and red; tends to do well in shaded display areas and is popular as a tang grazing supplement. Slow-growing relative to most export macros.
careNotes: Leafy Ogo (Halymenia maculata) is a red macroalgae in family Halymeniaceae, order Halymeniales — the same family as sibling species Dragon's Breath (H. durvillei) and Dragon's Tongue (H. dilatata). Its relatively thick, broadly lobed blades show a distinctively blotchy pink-to-red-to-purple coloration; blade edges may be slightly frilly but are typically broader and stiffer than the filamentous Ogo species it is sometimes confused with. TRADE NAME NOTE: The 'Ogo' trade name is shared across two unrelated genera in the hobby — Gracilaria spp. (Red Ogo, Green Ogo, Thick Ogo, etc.) and Halymenia spp. (Leafy Ogo, this species). Buyers should confirm the scientific name; Gracilaria Ogos tend to be faster-growing and more branched, while Halymenia Ogos are broad-bladed and slow-growing. Mosaic Macros sells this species as 'Purple Dragon's Breath' (not to be confused with Dragon's Breath, which is H. durvillei); Mosaic notes it is one of the few genuinely purple-looking species in the hobby and flags it as VERY sensitive to shipping, requiring overnight delivery for live arrival guarantees. Generally advised to maintain temperatures around 25.6–28.3°C (78–83°F), pH 7.8–8.4, SG 1.023–1.025, and alkalinity 8–12 dKH. AlgaeBarn notes the species tolerates slightly lower light intensities and can be placed in shadier or deeper parts of the tank compared to other display macros — medium-intensity lighting is generally sufficient. Moderate to high water flow is typically recommended. It tends to grow free-floating or holdfast-attached; maximum diameter has been reported around 18 cm (7 in). Herbivorous fish (tangs, rabbitfish) and sea urchins have been known to consume it readily, making it useful as a supplemental grazing food but risky in tanks with dedicated herbivores. As a photosynthetic organism it does not require direct feeding; trace element supplementation may support coloration in some systems. Propagation in the aquarium hobby is primarily by fragmentation. AlgaeBase CAPTCHA prevented direct database access during this research pass; taxonomy confirmed via Wikipedia (Halymeniaceae / Halymeniales) and is consistent with AlgaeBarn's species labeling. Per Mosaic care guide guidance: as a red or brown alga, this species will MELT at the base if buried in sand — attach to rock via superglue, zip-tie/fishing line, or wedging instead. (Sand-bed acceptance per Mosaic is limited to Caulerpa, Halimeda, Penicillus, Rhipocephalus, and Udotea genera, plus some calcified Dasycladales.)

additional.name: Mosaic Macros
additional.notes: Confirmed species as H. maculata; noted extreme shipping sensitivity requiring overnight shipping for live arrival guarantee; described as 'one of the only true purple-looking species in the hobby'. Limited husbandry parameter data on product page.

additional.name: Wikipedia — Halymenia
additional.notes: Confirmed genus taxonomy: family Halymeniaceae, order Halymeniales, class Florideophyceae. H. maculata listed as a recognized species (J. Agardh). AlgaeBase was inaccessible (CAPTCHA) during this research pass.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros general macroalgae care guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source — provides authoritative Mosaic-wide parameter targets (temp 22-26°C, pH 8.1-8.4, Ca 400-450 ppm, Mg 1250-1350 ppm, NO3 5-20 ppm, PO4 0.02-0.1 ppm) and the 4-method propagation standard. Added retroactively 2026-06-08.
